meta.description: Run a submission-readiness preflight on a manuscript before arXiv upload. Detects LLM artifacts, hallucinated references, placeholder content, AI-use disclosure gaps, and arXiv moderation risks.

functional_suitability.note: All promised use cases covered; MVP scope section explicitly delineates what is and is not supported

reliability.note: Graceful network degradation, PDF fallback, unresolved-include warnings; error messages could be slightly more actionable

performance_context.note: SKILL.md concise at ~70 lines; references loaded on-demand; minor inefficiency in sequential 4-script pipeline

agent_usability.note: Clear numbered workflow, consistent severity taxonomy, explicit 'When to read which reference' section; agent-assisted steps slightly less specified

human_usability.note: Comprehensive trigger phrases in description; PDF-only input degrades without clear user guidance on next steps

security.note: No hardcoded secrets, env var for email, pathlib for paths; file path input not explicitly sanitized against traversal

maintainability.note: Excellent modularity: data-driven pattern library, 4 independent scripts, clean JSON interfaces, eval fixtures with assertions

agent_specific.note: Precise trigger, perfect progressive disclosure, idempotent scripts, clear escape hatches; composability slightly limited by full-orchestration assumption

inputs.note: Verbatim/lstlisting/comment suppression works; one false positive on inline backtick-quoted mention

assertions.note: META-01 triggered on backtick-quoted as an AI language model in body text

key_strengths: Data-driven pattern library parsed at runtime from markdown tables — adding new patterns requires no code change
Excellent modularity: 4 independent scripts with clean JSON interfaces, 4 reference docs, eval fixtures with assertions
Conservative design philosophy: minimizes false positives, never calls papers AI-generated from style alone
Graceful network degradation: marks sections INCOMPLETE rather than silently skipping, prevents false PASS
Comprehensive trigger description covers natural user phrasings without over-triggering

recommendations.title: Inline-quoted AI phrases produce false positives
recommendations.problem: Papers discussing LLM detection that quote AI phrases in backticks (e.g. ``as an AI language model'') trigger BLOCKER findings.
recommendations.root_cause: strip_comments_and_verbatim() handles block-level suppression but not inline LaTeX quotes (backtick pairs).
recommendations.fix: Add a post-scan filter that suppresses findings where the match falls within LaTeX inline-quote delimiters (`` ... '') or within a sentence that is clearly meta-discussion about the pattern itself.

recommendations.title: PDF fallback has no cite-key extraction path
recommendations.problem: When pdftotext is unavailable, the skill cannot extract any text and therefore cannot run reference or artifact checks at all.
recommendations.root_cause: extract_manuscript_text.py returns empty raw_text when pdftotext is missing, with no alternative extraction method.
recommendations.fix: Consider adding a pure-Python PDF text extraction fallback (e.g. PyPDF2 or pdfminer.six) as an optional dependency, or document more prominently that LaTeX source is strongly preferred.

recommendations.title: DISC-01 severity could be calibrated per field
recommendations.problem: The disclosure check fires at MEDIUM for all fields equally, but arXiv policy notes indicate ML/NLP venues expect more explicit disclosure than pure-math.
recommendations.root_cause: scan_ai_artifacts.py uses a single MEDIUM severity for DISC-01 regardless of detected field or venue.
recommendations.fix: Add optional field/venue detection (from arXiv category in metadata or keywords) to calibrate DISC-01 severity: HIGH for ML/NLP/bio, LOW for math/physics where polish-only use is non-reportable.
